title: silene muscipula var. arvensis
intro: Silene muscipula var. arvensis, also known as catchfly or sticky catchfly, is a flowering plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Silene muscipula var. arvensis include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and combat fungal infections. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into tinctures, infusions, or salves, and can be used topically to treat minor cuts and scrapes, as well as internally to support immune function.
